verses.latin_vulgate: Et adjecit Dominus, et locutus est ad Job :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֖עַן יְהֹוָ֥ה אֶת־אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.king_james: Moreover the Lord answered Job, and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid qui contendit cum Deo, tam facile conquiescit ?  utique qui arguit Deum, debet respondere ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲ֭רֹב עִם־שַׁדַּ֣י יִסּ֑וֹר מוֹכִ֖יחַ אֱל֣וֹהַּ יַעֲנֶֽנָּה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Shall he that contendeth with the Almighty instruct him? he that reproveth God, let him answer it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Job Domino, dixit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֖עַן אִיּ֥וֹב אֶת־יְהֹוָ֗ה וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.king_james: Then Job answered the Lord, and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui leviter locutus sum, respondere quid possum ?  manum meam ponam super os meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֣ן קַ֭לֹּתִי מָ֣ה אֲשִׁיבֶ֑ךָּ יָ֝דִ֗י שַׂ֣מְתִּי לְמוֹ־פִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Behold, I am vile; what shall I answer thee? I will lay mine hand upon my mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unum locutus sum, quod utinam non dixissem :  et alterum, quibus ultra non addam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַחַ֣ת דִּ֭בַּרְתִּי וְלֹ֣א אֶעֱנֶ֑ה וּ֝שְׁתַּ֗יִם וְלֹ֣א אוֹסִֽיף׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Once have I spoken; but I will not answer: yea, twice; but I will proceed no further.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Dominus Job de turbine, dixit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעַן־יְהֹוָ֣ה אֶת־אִ֭יּוֹב (מנסערה) [מִ֥ן ׀ סְעָרָ֗ה] וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.king_james: Then answered the Lord unto Job out of the whirlwind, and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Accinge sicut vir lumbos tuos :  interrogabo te, et indica mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱזׇר־נָ֣א כְגֶ֣בֶר חֲלָצֶ֑יךָ אֶ֝שְׁאָלְךָ֗ וְהוֹדִיעֵֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: Gird up thy loins now like a man: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid irritum facies judicium meum,  et condemnabis me, ut tu justificeris ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַ֭אַף תָּפֵ֣ר מִשְׁפָּטִ֑י תַּ֝רְשִׁיעֵ֗נִי לְמַ֣עַן תִּצְדָּֽק׃
verses.king_james: Wilt thou also disannul my judgment? wilt thou condemn me, that thou mayest be righteous?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si habes brachium sicut Deus ?  et si voce simili tonas ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִם־זְר֖וֹעַ כָּאֵ֥ל ׀ לָ֑ךְ וּ֝בְק֗וֹל כָּמֹ֥הוּ תַרְעֵֽם׃
verses.king_james: Hast thou an arm like God? or canst thou thunder with a voice like him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Circumda tibi decorem, et in sublime erigere,  et esto gloriosus, et speciosis induere vestibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲדֵ֥ה נָ֣א גָא֣וֹן וָגֹ֑בַהּ וְה֖וֹד וְהָדָ֣ר תִּלְבָּֽשׁ׃
verses.king_james: Deck thyself now with majesty and excellency; and array thyself with glory and beauty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Disperge superbos in furore tuo,  et respiciens omnem arrogantem humilia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָ֭פֵץ עֶבְר֣וֹת אַפֶּ֑ךָ וּרְאֵ֥ה כׇל־גֵּ֝אֶ֗ה וְהַשְׁפִּילֵֽהוּ׃
verses.king_james: Cast abroad the rage of thy wrath: and behold every one that is proud, and abase him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respice cunctos superbos, et confunde eos,  et contere impios in loco suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רְאֵ֣ה כׇל־גֵּ֭אֶה הַכְנִיעֵ֑הוּ וַהֲדֹ֖ךְ רְשָׁעִ֣ים תַּחְתָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Look on every one that is proud, and bring him low; and tread down the wicked in their place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Absconde eos in pulvere simul,  et facies eorum demerge in foveam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טׇמְנֵ֣ם בֶּעָפָ֣ר יָ֑חַד פְּ֝נֵיהֶ֗ם חֲבֹ֣שׁ בַּטָּמֽוּן׃
verses.king_james: Hide them in the dust together; and bind their faces in secret.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego confitebor  quod salvare te possit dextera tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְגַם־אֲנִ֥י אוֹדֶ֑ךָּ כִּֽי־תוֹשִׁ֖עַ לְךָ֣ יְמִינֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Then will I also confess unto thee that thine own right hand can save thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce behemoth quem feci tecum,  fœnum quasi bos comedet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּה־נָ֣א בְ֭הֵמוֹת אֲשֶׁר־עָשִׂ֣יתִי עִמָּ֑ךְ חָ֝צִ֗יר כַּבָּקָ֥ר יֹאכֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: Behold now behemoth, which I made with thee; he eateth grass as an ox.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fortitudo ejus in lumbis ejus,  et virtus illius in umbilico ventris ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּה־נָ֣א כֹח֣וֹ בְמׇתְנָ֑יו וְ֝אוֹנ֗וֹ בִּשְׁרִירֵ֥י בִטְנֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Lo now, his strength is in his loins, and his force is in the navel of his belly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stringit caudam suam quasi cedrum ;  nervi testiculorum ejus perplexi sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַחְפֹּ֣ץ זְנָב֣וֹ כְמוֹ־אָ֑רֶז גִּידֵ֖י פַחֲדָ֣ו יְשֹׂרָֽגוּ׃
verses.king_james: He moveth his tail like a cedar: the sinews of his stones are wrapped together.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ossa ejus velut fistulæ æris ;  cartilago illius quasi laminæ ferreæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲ֭צָמָיו אֲפִיקֵ֣י נְחֻשָׁ֑ה גְּ֝רָמָ֗יו כִּמְטִ֥יל בַּרְזֶֽל׃
verses.king_james: His bones are as strong pieces of brass; his bones are like bars of iron.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse est principium viarum Dei :  qui fecit eum applicabit gladium ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֭וּא רֵאשִׁ֣ית דַּרְכֵי־אֵ֑ל הָ֝עֹשׂ֗וֹ יַגֵּ֥שׁ חַרְבּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: He is the chief of the ways of God: he that made him can make his sword to approach unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Huic montes herbas ferunt :  omnes bestiæ agri ludent ibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־ב֭וּל הָרִ֣ים יִשְׂאוּ־ל֑וֹ וְֽכׇל־חַיַּ֥ת הַ֝שָּׂדֶ֗ה יְשַׂחֲקוּ־שָֽׁם׃
verses.king_james: Surely the mountains bring him forth food, where all the beasts of the field play.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sub umbra dormit in secreto calami,  et in locis humentibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּֽחַת־צֶאֱלִ֥ים יִשְׁכָּ֑ב בְּסֵ֖תֶר קָנֶ֣ה וּבִצָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: He lieth under the shady trees, in the covert of the reed, and fens.

verses.latin_vulgate: Protegunt umbræ umbram ejus :  circumdabunt eum salices torrentis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְסֻכֻּ֣הוּ צֶאֱלִ֣ים צִֽלְל֑וֹ יְ֝סֻבּ֗וּהוּ עַרְבֵי־נָֽחַל׃
verses.king_james: The shady trees cover him with their shadow; the willows of the brook compass him about.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce absorbebit fluvium, et non mirabitur,  et habet fiduciam quod influat Jordanis in os ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֤ן יַעֲשֹׁ֣ק נָ֭הָר לֹ֣א יַחְפּ֑וֹז יִבְטַ֓ח ׀ כִּֽי־יָגִ֖יחַ יַרְדֵּ֣ן אֶל־פִּֽיהוּ׃
verses.king_james: Behold, he drinketh up a river, and hasteth not: he trusteth that he can draw up Jordan into his mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: In oculis ejus quasi hamo capiet eum,  et in sudibus perforabit nares ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּעֵינָ֥יו יִקָּחֶ֑נּוּ בְּ֝מוֹקְשִׁ֗ים יִנְקׇב־אָֽף׃
verses.king_james: He taketh it with his eyes: his nose pierceth through snares.
